title: Java AWS Developer
description:
We now have a new requirement and are looking for a consultant colleague with the qualifications listed below. The position is part of our consulting business, which means you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments.

*As part of your application, you may be presented to clients/partners as part of the recruitment process. If selected for the assignment, you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments as part of our consulting business.*

 **Your Profile (Must-have requirements)**  

* Experienced cloud developer with expertise in DevOps, Java, Grafana, Kafka, AWS and related technologies.
* Proven history of integrating complex systems in cloud environments, with a systematic approach to optimizing performance.
* Familiarity with cloud security in distributed systems.
* Experience in event-driven architecture.
* Experience using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to automate and manage cloud infrastructure.
* We value experience in energy systems.
* Passionate about sustainability, with a strong analytical mindset, working in an organized, structured way.
* Driven by curiosity; continuously seek improvement, challenge the status quo, and explore new technologies.
* Communicate well, thrive in collaborative, fast-paced environments, and embrace working in diverse teams.
* Over 5 years of experience and a bachelor's degree (or equivalent knowledge).
* Driver's license (level B) is required.

  

**Application deadline:** 27-05-2026 **About Us**  

We currently employ experienced consultants in areas such as System Development, AI, Machine Learning, Testing, Cloud, Infra DevOps, and IT Project Management.  

  

Working at Deploja is something entirely different from working at an ordinary consulting company—and we are really proud of that. So, what makes us unique?  

  

We simply offer more than just a high salary and greater freedom; that’s just the beginning. With us, you’ll find the work environment and opportunities you deserve. Welcome to Deploja, where the grass is indeed greener!

 **Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**


Exciting assignments with our many clients. We focus on long-term projects, which gives you the opportunity to create value for the client while also developing yourself.


A secure monthly salary – together we’ll agree on a base salary depending on your assignments.


Company car – we encourage you to choose an electric car for a more sustainable future.


Occupational pension and great opportunities for salary conversion.


Vacation – Would you like more than 30 days of vacation, perhaps 60 days?


Private health insurance, accident insurance, and life insurance for increased security.


Skills development – you set your own budget and choose the training you wish to attend.


Quality of life – Are you struggling to balance everyday life and wish to work a little less while still earning as much, if not more?

*We handle applications continuously, and due to the high workload, it's especially important to include a short motivation for the position, explaining how you meet the requirements.*

title: Fullstack Developer
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.


We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a **Fullstack Developer** in our **Stockholm** office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?

### **The Role**


Join our Attract team – the team responsible for creating the first impression of Viaplay. We build and optimize landing pages, campaign experiences, and product pages across all our markets. Our focus is on creating fast, engaging, and high-performing user experiences that turn visitors into customers.

**What You’ll Do**

* Build and maintain modern web applications using React, Next.js, and Node.js
* Work across the stack to create smooth and performant user experiences also meeting our markets different needs
* Collaborate with other product teams, designers, product managers, and analysts
* Contribute to experiments and improvements based on user behavior and data
* Help improve performance, SEO, and tracking

**Tech Stack**

**Web:** TypeScript, React, Next.js

**Content and data integrations:** Sanity, internal APIs, JSON-based data flows

**Cloud, build and delivery:** CloudFront, AWS S3, Lambda, GitHub

### **What we’re looking for**


We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following:


* Experience building web applications with JavaScript/TypeScript
* Experience with React and interest in modern webb development
* Experience with CI/CD workflows &Data transformation pipelines
* Experience of of APIs and cloud environments preferably AWS
* A collaborative mindset and willingness to learn

**It’s a bonus if you have:**

* Experience with Next.js
* Interest in performance, SEO, or A/B testing
* Experience in working with AI agents, agent workflows, or prompt/harness engineering

### **What we offer you**

* **We’ve got you covered!** 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
* **Your wellbeing matters.** We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
* **Flexibility that works.** We follow a hybrid model with at least three days a week in the office, giving you the best mix of collaboration and focus time.
* **A safe space to grow and up-skill.** Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
* **An innovative environment with Hack Days once a year.** This week-long initiative allows you to think outside the box and deliver creative, technical solutions that (more often than not) go on to be implemented, either in our product or our ways of working.
* **Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do.** So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including serier & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.

### **Hit play today**


If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position.
  

  

Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: Senior Frontend Developer
description:
*Natural Cycles° is a leading women’s health company that developed the world’s first birth control app, used by millions globally. We are a fast growing startup, and we promote an international working environment filled with smart and ambitious colleagues based in Stockholm, Geneva, New York, and remotely. Our mission is to pioneer women’s health through research and passion, empowering every woman with the knowledge she needs to take charge of her health.*


We're looking for a Senior Frontend Engineer to join our Product Growth team with a focus on optimizing the web sign-up funnel. In this role, you will take ownership of the technical direction of our web sign-up experience – shipping high-quality, accessible, and performant user interfaces, and helping more people discover and get started with Natural Cycles.


You will play a key role in shaping the first touchpoints of our web experience, working in a fast-paced, data-informed environment where your code has a direct and visible impact on conversion and growth. You will also be a technical mentor for junior engineers on the team and contribute to raising the bar across the codebase.

**Please note that we are hiring for this role in Sweden.**

 **What you will be doing**

* Owning and driving the technical delivery of the web-based sign-up funnel, from architecture decisions to shipping polished user interfaces.
* Building and running A/B experiments, iterating rapidly based on real-time results and data insights.
* Setting the standard for code quality, performance, and accessibility within the team.
* Mentoring and supporting junior engineers through code reviews, pairing, and technical guidance.
* Collaborating closely with product managers, designers, and analysts to deliver user-facing features of the highest quality.
* Contributing to a healthy, scalable, and sustainable codebase.
* Working hands-on with analytics and data, taking a data-driven approach to frontend decisions wherever possible.
* Ensuring continuous delivery of new releases while working in an agile way.

**What skills and experience we think you have**

* 5+ years of professional experience in frontend development, with a strong portfolio of shipped web products.
* Experience working with backend or full-stack development
* Deep expertise in TypeScript and the Angular framework (or equivalent experience with another modern component-based framework and the ability to ramp up quickly).
* A strong command of modern CSS – Flexbox, Grid, and experience with pre/post-processors such as SASS or PostCSS.
* Solid experience building responsive, performant, accessible, and inclusive user interfaces.
* Experience with A/B testing and conversion rate optimization.
* Comfort working with data and analytics tools to inform and validate decisions.
* Experience with Git, GitHub, pull requests, and conducting thorough code reviews.
* The ability to communicate clearly and collaborate effectively across disciplines.

**It's a plus if you also have**

* Leadership experience
* Background in growth-focused product teams or experience with web sign-up funnels.
* Commercial acumen and an understanding of business drivers.
* A passion for women's health.

**What we offer**

* Flexible work arrangement - you will be part of a team based in and around Stockholm that values effective collaboration and transparent communication, irrespective of work location
* Professional development - you will collaborate with industry leading developers, promoting continuous growth and skill enhancement
* Modern technology - you will leverage innovative technologies and tools, within an environment that empowers you to contribute ideas and take ownership of your work
* Impactful projects - you will contribute to groundbreaking projects that redefine industry standards and create tangible value
* Commitment to quality - you will join a dynamic and progressive organization that prioritizes profitable, long-term product development

 **Location**


We are remote-friendly, but we find great value in being able to connect with our teams in person. **Most of the Product Growth team you will work with is located in Stockholm, and the team occasionally meets in the office for collaboration and face-to-face time.**

 **Sounds interesting?**


If you want to be part of a successful team, we encourage you to apply for this position as soon as possible.


We look forward to hearing from you!

 **How to apply**


To apply, just upload your CV and answer the questions on the application form. Keep in mind that we can't accept applications through email because of GDPR, and only applications submitted via the career site (and in English) will be considered.


At Natural Cycles we value diversity and inclusion because we know that teams with people from different backgrounds and experiences are stronger. We welcome candidates from all walks of life and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates.  

  

**Note: We are not looking for consultants or help from recruitment agencies**

title: Fullstackutvecklare till snabbväxande psykologföretag
description:
Är du en fullstackutvecklare som gillar att ta ansvar hela vägen från idé till produktion? Vill du arbeta i ett team där teknik, design och användarnytta hänger ihop på riktigt? Då kan det här vara rätt roll för dig.


Vi söker nu en driven och kvalitetsmedveten fullstackutvecklare med stark kompetens inom PHP (gärna Laravel) och Vue.js. Hos oss får du arbeta med att bygga bra systemlösningar, hemsidor och webbapplikationer som i slutändan hjälper människor att må bättre. Vi ser att du med din kompetens kan komplettera och förbättra ditt resultat med AI för att snabbt och säkert leverera bra produkter.

##### **Om rollen**


I rollen arbetar du genom hela utvecklingskedjan, från backendarkitektur och databaser till responsiva och tillgängliga användargränssnitt. Du kommer att vara delaktig i både tekniska beslut och vidareutveckling av produkter med höga krav på kvalitet, prestanda och användarupplevelse.

##### **Du kommer bland annat att arbeta med**

* Backend-utveckling i Laravel/PHP med fokus på stabil API-arkitektur
* Frontend-utveckling med Vue.js och modern JavaScript
* Databasdesign, optimering och integrationer mot externa system
* Implementering av responsiva gränssnitt med HTML5, CSS/SCSS och Tailwind
* Tillgänglighetsanpassning enligt WCAG
* Arbete i och med etablerade designsystem

##### **Vi söker dig som**

* Har flera års erfarenhet av Laravel och Vue.js
* Har erfarenhet av arbete i Wordpress
* Skriver ren, strukturerad och vältestad kod
* Har god förståelse för säkerhet, prestanda och skalbar systemdesign
* Är van vid API-first-arkitektur och moderna utvecklingsflöden
* Har ett öga för UX, tillgänglighet och responsiv design
* Trivs i mindre team där samarbete och ansvarstagande är viktigt
* Kommunicerar tydligt och gillar att lösa problem tillsammans med andra

##### **Meriterande erfarenheter**

* Erfarenhet av WCAG 3-tillgänglighet
* Vana att jobba med och bidra till designsystem
* Tidigare erfarenhet av integrationer mot tredjepartssystem

##### **Därför kommer du att trivas hos oss**

* Moderna projekt med fokus på kvalitet framför snabbhack
* Möjlighet att skapa saker som faktiskt betyder något för människor
* Ett sammansvetsat team med hög kompetens och prestigelöst samarbete
* Flexibilitet och autonomi i ditt arbete
* Stor variation i projekt och tekniska utmaningar

##### **Om Svea KBT & Elly Care**


Utan hjälp av externa investerare, har vi gått från 5 till 120+ medarbetare och 17 kliniker runt om i Sverige de senaste fyra åren. Och det är just för att vi alla engagerar oss så otroligt mycket i värdet vi skapar: psykisk hälsa. Psykologer, läkare och sjuksköterskor arbetar sida vid sida med ett verksamhetsteam som ständigt utvecklar, förbättrar och driver verksamheten framåt.


Svea KBT och Elly Care är vi idag en av Sveriges ledande aktörer inom psykisk hälsa. Vi arbetar brett, från terapi och medicinsk behandling till neuropsykiatriska utredningar, företagshälsa, utbildningar och handledning. Samtidigt bygger vi hela tiden vidare: utvecklar nya tjänster, vässar våra arbetssätt och hittar smartare sätt att nå och hjälpa fler. Vi har en unik och spännande resa framför oss!


Vi är ett team som gillar tempo, ansvar och utveckling. Här finns mycket kompetens, men noll prestige. Alla gör sitt jobb ordentligt, bryr sig på riktigt och drivs av samma sak: att hjälpa fler att må bättre.

##### **Praktisk information**

* **Placering:** Stockholm, på kontor min. 60%
* **Omfattning:** Heltid
* **Start:** Enligt överenskommelse


Låter det intressant? Skicka gärna in din ansökan och berätta lite om dig själv, projekt du arbetat med och vad du vill utvecklas vidare inom.

title: Senior Fullstack TypeScript Engineer
description:
**About the role** 




 We're looking for a senior fullstack engineer to join our team building the WtR Platform. 
 



  





 With 15+ years in commercial real estate, we're building an ecosystem of modern digital tools (WtR Platform) that's redefining and streamlining how office space is communicated and leased. A combination of high-touch-high-tech. Today's leasing process is manual, slow, and stuck in the past — we're setting a new digital standard for the industry. Our clients are some of the world's largest commercial real estate companies, including Skanska, Tishman Speyer, Vasakronan, Fabege, Hines, and Castellum.
 



  





 You'll join a small, AI-enabled team and work across a modern TypeScript monorepo, shipping APIs, platform features, and the tooling that ties it all together. No bloated processes. No unnecessary meetings. The code and the vision speak for themselves.
 



  





 We're looking for someone best-in-class — a developer who cares deeply about how a product feels, believes in simplicity and elegance, and wants to move fast with AI as a force multiplier.
 



  





**What you'll work on** 



* API development — Building and extending our Effect-TS based API with PostgreSQL, Drizzle ORM, and fine-grained authorization (Ory Keto)
* Platform features — Full-stack work across our Vinxi/React 19 applications with TanStack Router, React Query, and type-safe API clients (OpenAPI, tRPC)
* Real-time collaboration — Yjs/CRDT-based document editing with PartyServer WebSocket infrastructure
* Infrastructure & DX — Cloud-native services on AWS (Fargate, RDS, S3, SES) deployed via FlightControl, frontend on Vercel, with OpenTelemetry observability
* Internal tooling — Custom CLI tools, workspace management, and shared packages in a pnpm + Turborepo monorepo



  





**What we're looking for** 



* Strong TypeScript skills — you're comfortable with generics, branded types, discriminated unions, and type-safe API patterns
* Experience with or interest in functional programming — our backend uses Effect-TS extensively (services, dependency injection, error handling, concurrency)
* Solid understanding of PostgreSQL — schema design, migrations, query optimization
* Familiarity with React and modern frontend patterns (server components, signals, state machines)
* Comfortable in a monorepo environment with shared packages, workspace tooling, and CI pipelines
* Bonus: experience with real-time/collaborative systems (CRDTs, WebSockets), 3D rendering, or geospatial work



  





**How we work** 



* All work refined, planned, and executed in Linear
* TypeScript everywhere — frontend to backend to CLI tooling
* Type safety at every boundary — OpenAPI specs, Zod schemas, Effect services
* Custom internal tooling
* Domain-driven API design with repository and policy patterns
* Automated quality gates: linting, formatting, type checking, architecture boundary enforcement



  





  





**Remote first** 




 We're a remote-first team. You can work from wherever you do your best thinking — home, a café, a co-working space, or a different time zone entirely. We keep a reasonable overlap with European working hours (roughly CET ± a few hours) so collaboration, pairing, and the occasional synchronous design discussion stay easy, but we don't watch the clock. We optimize for written communication, clear documentation, and async decision-making, with video calls reserved for the moments they genuinely help. A couple of times a year we get the whole team together in person for a few days of hacking, planning, and good food.
 



  





 Being based in the Stockholm region is a plus — part of the team is here, so it makes it easier to grab coffee, pair in person now and then, or join the occasional in-office day — but it's genuinely not a requirement.
 



  





**Benefits** 




 Beyond salary, we also want to make sure our team enjoys working with us.
 


* Wellness allowance
* Flexible working hours
* Office option available, but remote-first if preferred



  





  





**About Walk the Room** 




  





 Walk The Room is a 15-year-old company reshaping how commercial real estate is presented and leased. Our Studio is the creative bespoke arm — producing high-end visuals and 3D experiences that bring future buildings and spaces to life for property owners. Our Platform (WtR Solutions) is the tech side: an AI-driven ecosystem of leasing tools built for portfolios with high tenant movement, giving property owners a seamless, interactive way to lease their spaces. Together, they help the industry move from static brochures to immersive, data-driven experiences.

title: Frontend Software Engineer
description:
**Description**
 Welcome to SSC Space (formerly Swedish Space Corporation), the perfect workplace for those passionate about technology in general and space in particular.
   

  

 We offer specialized expertise in satellite communications, spacecraft consulting services, and rocket and balloon systems. SSC Space has one of the world’s largest global networks of ground stations for satellite communications, enabling customers to achieve their spacecraft mission goals. We own and operate the Esrange Space Center in Kiruna, northern Sweden.
   

  

 We are now looking for front-end engineers who are passionate about creating excellent user experiences and writing high-quality, maintainable code. This is a unique opportunity to help define and build our internal and client-facing platforms.
   

  

**Requirements**
 As an engineer at SSC Space, you will work on implementing new features and help shape the technical approach to our front-end stack. We value engineers who enjoy solving challenging problems, delivering practical solutions, and growing their expertise in a collaborative environment.
   

  

**What We're Looking For**
* B.S. Computer Science or Computer/Software Engineering, or equivalent experience.
* Deep experience with React and its eco system, leveraging modern paradigms such as Hooks, Context API, and Redux to build scalable user interfaces.
* Exposure to full-stack development with a willingness to work across the stack.
* Experience developing and querying RESTful APIs.
* Strong communication and teamwork abilities.
* Minimum 3-5 years of relevant working experience.


**Additional Experience We Value**
* Proficiency or interest in learning new languages, specifically C-oriented languages or Go.
* Solid understanding of database design, optimization, and management.
* Experience with software development best practices including Scrum, DevOps and CI/CD Methodologies.
* Experience with containerization and orchestration tools such as Docker and Kubernetes.
* Interest in or experience working with AI-assisted development tools such as GitHub Copilot, agentic AI workflows, or similar.
* Understanding of microservice architecture.
* Experience in the space industry.


**Responsibilities**
 We believe you take ownership of your work and care about building reliable, well-functioning solutions for both users and your team. You combine analytical thinking with a practical approach to problem solving. You take ownership of your work, drive tasks forward, and are comfortable working with complex technical details. You can work independently when needed, but also collaborate effectively with others. You have experience contributing to production applications and value writing maintainable, reliable code.
   

  

**As a Frontend Software Engineer, You Will**
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design and develop user-facing applications supporting a range of mission-critical systems.
* Contribute to the modernization and migration of legacy systems into scalable, maintainable architectures.
* Build and maintain high-quality, performant code for both new and existing applications.
* Improve system observability through implementation of monitoring, logging, and alerting solutions.
* Support internal tools and platforms related to scheduling, antenna control, orbital launch services, and emerging technologies.
* Enhance development workflows through automated testing and streamlined deployment processes.
* Promote and contribute to agile methodologies, fostering continuous improvement within the team.
* Support and deliver reliable solutions for customer missions.


**Benefits**
 WE OFFER YOU
   

  

* A unique opportunity to contribute to a wide range of projects and play an active role in both the Swedish and international space industry.
* An excellent opportunity for personal and career development.
* An exciting, international workplace characterized by an open atmosphere and proud employees all over the world.


**Additional Benefits**
* In addition to vacation and traditional public holidays, we offer extra paid leave throughout the year, such as time off between Christmas and New Year, Ascension Day, amounting to 6-11 extra days depending on the calendar.
* Wellness allowance of SEK 5,000 per year.
* Health benefits such as voluntary health insurance and regular health checks for eligible employees.
* Access to a benefit bike through a tax-efficient leasing program.
* Support for employee-driven activities that promote connection, health, and culture across teams.
* Collective Agreement, including good pensions plans, parental pay and insurance.


 LOCATION
   

  

 This is a full-time, permanent position based at our office in either Solna or Kiruna.
   

  

 FURTHER INFORMATION
   

  

 For questions regarding employment terms or union-related matters, you are welcome to contact our union representatives:
   

  

 Sveriges Ingenjörer: SverigesIngenjorer-AF@sscspace.com
   

  

 Unionen: unionen@sscspace.com
   

  

  

  

 NEXT STEP
   

  

 If this opportunity sparks your interest, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible! We review applications continuously and will conduct interviews throughout the process.
   

  

 You can submit your application up until 2026-05-31, which is the latest possible date for consideration.
   

  

 Please note, however, that the position may be filled before this date, and the advertisement may therefore close earlier. This allows us to manage the process efficiently and give each application the attention it deserves.
   

  

 When applying, please include your CV. While you are welcome to include a brief personal note or letter, our evaluation will primarily focus on your skills and qualifications as outlined in your CV, ensuring a fair and unbiased recruitment process.
   

  

 This position requires approved pre-employment screening. These controls are based on the role that you will fulfill within the company. Additional requirements regarding citizenship may apply.
   

  

 WE HELP EARTH BENEFIT FROM SPACE
   

  

 SSC Space is a leading global provider of advanced space services, with more than 50 years of experience. We help space organizations, research institutes, commercial and institutional actors from all over the world to get access to space.
   

  

 With local presence on all continents and about 750 committed employees, we offer specialist expertise in satellite communications and satellite control services, spacecraft operations, rocket and balloon systems, launch services and flight test services, as well as engineering, operations and consultancy services for space missions.
   

  

 We help Earth benefit from Space. Learn more at sscspace.com

title: Senior Backend Developer
description:
****Let's shape the future of renting together.****
****Who we are****
 Qasa is a fast-growing scale-up transforming the rental housing market across the Nordics. Since launching in 2014, we’ve been focused on making renting easier, safer, and more accessible for both landlords and tenants.
   

  

 What started as a small startup is now the leading rental platform in Sweden — and we’re just getting started. We’re expanding across the Nordics with a highly skilled, driven team passionate about building a product that makes a real difference in people’s lives.
   

  

 If you're excited about having a meaningful impact on a product that helps people find and rent homes, one of life’s most essential needs, then you might be the person we’re looking for.
   

  

 We’re now looking for another
 **senior Ruby backend developer** 
 to join our team and help shape the platform behind the rental experience for thousands of people across the Nordics.
   

  

****What will you do****
* Design and build our scalable backend system in Ruby on Rails
* Work closely with product, design, and other engineers to deliver features end-to-end
* Improve and maintain our API and internal services
* Contribute to architectural decisions and technical direction
* Take ownership of your work, from idea to production
* Mentor and inspire junior colleagues


****What you have****
* 10+ years of backend experience, preferably with Ruby and Ruby on Rails (or similar object-oriented languages like Python)
* A solid grasp of system design and architectural principles
* The ability to structure clean, modular, and scalable codebases
* A strong preference for writing maintainable, understandable code over clever abstractions
* Confidence in building libraries for internal use, when off-the-shelf libs fall short


****Who you are****
**You’re humble, collaborative** 
 , and thoughtful — someone who sees software development as a team sport. You don’t guard “your” code and are always ready to pair up, share ideas, and iterate together. You take pride in delivering quality work, but never at the expense of others.
   

  

 You thrive in a fast-moving environment, learn quickly, and enjoy challenging yourself. You’re driven to get things done — not because of pressure or competition, but because you care about your work's impact.
   

  

 You value clear communication, kind feedback, and supporting your teammates. The right mindset and a strong cultural fit are more important than checking every box on a tech list. If that sounds like you, you’ll feel right at home here.
   

  

 You’re self-driven and comfortable taking initiative, whether that means improving an existing system, suggesting a better approach, or picking up something that needs doing. You care about progress and quality, and you enjoy helping move both the product and the team forward.
   

  

****Why Qasa?****
* A supportive and ambitious team that cares about what we’re building
* A real product solving real problems — used by people every day
* Flexible work: join us in our bright, modern office at Medborgarplatsen in central Stockholm
* Competitive salary and benefits
* A chance to make a meaningful impact in a growing company


 Sound like a good fit? We’d love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out if you have questions — we’re happy to chat.
   

  

**Tech stack**
* Ruby 4.0
* Rails 8.1
* Minitest, Inertia+React
* Postgres, Redis, ElasticSearch, GoodJob
* GitHub, AWS, Honeybadger


**Hiring process**
* Initial screening call – a brief conversation about your background, experience, and what you’re looking for (~30 minutes)
* Technical interview with the Hiring Manager – a deeper dive into your technical experience, including hands-on pairing (~1.5 hours, on-site)
* Culture interview with our CTO and our CPO – a discussion of values, collaboration, and how we work together (~1 hour, on-site)
* Offer and reference checks


**Perks And Benefits**
* Central location – Right in the middle of Södermalm in Stockholm. This is a place for you to find working peace without ever being more than one elevator ride away from the pulse of the city.
* Generous wellness grant – We provide you with a generous wellness grant (Friskvårdsbidrag). If you feel good, the company feels good. Simple as that.
* All equipment you need – We supply you with quality equipment when you work with us. Let us know what you need to thrive, and we will get it for you.
* Open door afterwork – On Fridays, we get together for a beer or two. Feel free to invite friends!
* Collaborative Community – We work and play together. We help each other to reach our goals. Together we are stronger.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.
   

  

 1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to
 **$100M+ in ARR** 
 , with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.
   

  

 We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.
   

  

 Joining Legora means three things.
   

  

* We lean in: ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
* We fight for excellence: high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
* We grow together: as a team and with our customers.


 Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.
   

  

 If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.
   

  

**The role**
 As a Software Engineer at Legora, you’ll play a key role in building the technical foundation that powers our products, designing, developing, and improving the core systems used by legal professionals around the world. You’ll work across the stack, contributing to the architecture and features that define our platform.
   

  

* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**What You Will Be Doing**
* Lead the design, development, and deployment of end-to-end features for our web platform, ensuring high performance, security, and reliability.
* Take full ownership of critical parts of the codebase, balancing quality, scalability, and speed of development.
* Work with technologies such as React, Node.js, AWS, Azure, and Kubernetes, leveraging the right tools to solve complex challenges.
* Collaborate closely with product and design teams to translate requirements into elegant, robust technical solutions.
* Review code and mentor colleagues, raising the overall technical bar and fostering a culture of excellence.
* Drive continuous improvement by challenging assumptions, exploring new technologies, and pushing both yourself and the team to innovate.
* Contribute to infrastructure, integrations, and architecture discussions to ensure the platform evolves sustainably.


**Who You Are**
* An engineer with experience building and shipping modern software products.
* Comfortable working across the stack, with solid skills in backend development.
* Passionate about writing maintainable, secure code—and take pride in building things that last.
* A problem solver who enjoys thinking about architecture, data flows, and long-term sustainability.
* Familiar with cloud infrastructure, distributed systems, and integrations (experience with enterprise-scale systems is a plus, but not required).
* Proactive, curious, and motivated by working in a fast-paced, collaborative startup environment.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Software Developer at Monitum
description:
Do you want your code to create real-world impact – for people, business, and society? At Monitum, you’ll join a product-driven development team where your work directly enables new business opportunities and strengthens a trusted platform used by hundreds of thousands of users. Our solutions are used through channel partnerships with companies such as telecom operators, loan brokers, and insurance providers – creating both technical complexity and meaningful impact. This is an opportunity for a developer who enjoys combining technical excellence with a business mindset, while being part of something that truly matters.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Software Developer at Monitum, you’ll be a key contributor in a development team of 8 developers and part of a company of 24 colleagues in total. You’ll report to the Head of Development and work close to both product and business. Your main focus will be to build and ship new, high-impact functionality that drives customer value and supports the company’s growth in Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR).
   

  

 You will work across the full-stack and take shared ownership of the service layer, ensuring it remains secure, scalable, and sustainable as the product and organization grow. The role combines hands-on development with a strong focus on quality, security, and long-term maintainability.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities Include**
* Developing and launching new features across the full-stack (TypeScript, React, NestJS)
* Writing clean, maintainable code using TDD and robust automated testing
* Identifying and mitigating security risks in both application and infrastructure
* Collaborating closely with the team to continuously improve development workflows, tools, and architecture using technologies such as NX and Kubernetes


**YOUR PROFILE**
 You are a growth-oriented and self-driven developer who takes responsibility for the whole product, not just isolated pieces of code. You value collaboration highly and approach your work with a strong sense of accountability. Being humble comes naturally to you — you enjoy helping others and believe that the best results are achieved when the team succeeds together.
   

  

**You Have**
* 5+ years of professional experience in full-stack development
* Expert-level knowledge of TypeScript
* Strong backend experience with NestJS (or deep expertise in IOC containers and dependency injection)
* Professional experience with React
* A strong understanding of automated testing (integration and acceptance) and a TDD-first mindset
* A business-centric approach to development, understanding how technical decisions impact MRR and customer retention
* Fluency in English


**Bonus Points For**
* Knowledge of Kubernetes (k8s) and Helm
* Experience working with monorepos and the NX build system
* Proficiency in Swedish


**About Monitum**
 Monitum has over a decade of experience building secure, business-critical solutions through close collaboration between development, product, and business. We are a mature startup on a clear journey towards scale-up, with strong momentum, a stable product, and a clear long-term ambition.
   

  

 The culture is defined by high professionalism combined with genuine work joy. We value how you show up just as much as what you deliver, and we build teams with strong people, not just strong CVs. Collaboration, openness, and supporting each other in everyday work are core to how we succeed.
   

  

 You’ll be part of a dynamic environment with strong team spirit, close access to decision-making, and a shared motivation to contribute to something bigger than individual tasks.
   

  

**What We Offer**
* Competitive compensation and attractive benefits
* Flexibility and a healthy work-life balance thanks to a trust-based, sustainable approach to work
* A modern technical environment
* Continuous professional development in a close-knit, experienced team with real product impact


**Additional Information**
* Work Authorization: Sweden work authorization is a precondition of employment. Monitum will not consider candidates who require sponsorship for a work-authorized visa
* Relocation: No relocation assistance is provided for this role. Candidates must reside in Stockholm, Sweden


**YOUR APPLICATION**
 In this recruitment, Monitum is collaborating with Wise.
   

  

 We do not accept applications via email. Instead, you can easily apply with your CV or LinkedIn profile at wise.se. We use competency-based recruitment as our method, striving for a more potential-based match. We therefore welcome applicants of different backgrounds, ages and experiences, as we know that diversity enriches us and makes us stronger and smarter as an organization.
   

  

 If you have questions about the role or the recruitment process, please contact the responsible recruitment consultant: christian.nodelijk@wise.se.
   

  

 Interviews are conducted on an ongoing basis, and the position may be filled before the final application date.
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application.

title: Senior Backend Developer to Vertiseit
description:
Are you passionate about building scalable, cloud-native platforms? Join us in shaping the leading in-store experience management platform for global brands and leading retailers.
   

  

 At Vertiseit Group, we’re on a mission to become the world’s #1 platform company for digital in-store. We're now looking for a talented backend developer to join our team working at the heart of our technology - building and evolving the core platform that powers our business brands: Grassfish, Dise, and Visual Art.
   

  

****The Role****
 You’ll join a strong cross-functional development team focused on developing our
 **shared platform** 
 . It’s the backbone of our group’s software solutions (IXM Platforms), enabling modularity, speed, and innovation across brands - through microservices, API-first architecture, and cloud-native capabilities. We work with cutting-edge technologies and enjoy technology discussions and pair programming.
   

  

 In this role, you will:
   

  

* Design, develop, and optimize high-quality software solutions
* Ensure the reliability, scalability, and performance of our software
* Stay updated with industry trends and continuously improve our technology stack
* Contribute to technical discussions, code reviews, and knowledge-sharing within the team


**Technologies you’ll get to work with**
* C#
* .NET 10
* MS SQL Server and Entity Framework Core 10
* Communication protocols with REST API, SignalR and gRPC
* Unit testing using xUnit
* SaaS solution based on a microservices architecture on Azure Container Apps
* Azure services such as Azure Container Apps, Container Registry, Storage Account, SQL Server, Application Insights, Key Vault, App Configuration, Azure Functions etc.
* Infrastructure as Code via Terraform
* CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions


****Who You Are****
 We are looking for a passionate and experienced developer with a strong problem-solving mindset, a drive for innovation and a deep technical understanding. You possess a great deal of curiosity and like to learn and teach others. You are happy to work both individually and together with others. We would like to see that you have worked with the entire chain of a product life cycle.
   

  

 To succeed in this role, we see that you have:
   

  

* Several years hands-on experience as a software developer
* In-depth knowledge of building backend solutions with Microsoft technology stack
* Strong proficiency in modern programming languages and frameworks, such as C# and .NET
* Great experience in delivering well-tested, maintainable and functioning code
* Experience working with Cloud development, particularly Microsoft Azure Container Apps
* Solid knowledge of databases, APIs and system integrations
* A proactive and team-oriented mindset – you take ownership and drive development forward
* Strong communication skills in English


 Nice to have:
   

  

* Familiarity with DevOps practices such as CI/CD pipelines
* Strong communication skills in Swedish
* A good understanding of SQL, specifically T-SQL


****Why Join Vertiseit?****
 We offer an inclusive work environment where you will be surrounded by skilled and passionate colleagues.
   

  

* Well-being first – Enjoy health initiatives and a generous wellness allowance
* Team spirit & fun – We believe in having fun together and organizing activities to create great memories
* Learn & grow – Work alongside talented colleagues in a collaborative and knowledge-sharing environment


****Practical Information****
 📍
 **Location:** 
 Full-time position at our office in Varberg or Stockholm
   

  

 📅
 **Application deadline: May 15** 
 (We recruit on an ongoing basis, so apply as soon as possible!)
   

  

 ✉
 **Questions?** 
 Contact Hiring Manager Lisa Spjut at lisa.spjut@vertiseit.com
   

  

 You can submit your application in Swedish or English. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Python Developer - Remote in Sweden
description:
Anyone AI is recruiting skilled Python Developers to work on a project with a leading AI lab.
   

  

**Qualifications:**
* Advanced professional written proficiency in English
* 3–7 years of professional software engineering experience
* Strong proficiency in Python and JavaScript/TypeScript; working knowledge of Java, C#, or Go
* Backend or full‑stack development experience in production systems
* Experience with testing frameworks (e.g., pytest, Jest, JUnit, xUnit, Go testing)
* Proven ability to debug and navigate large, multi‑file codebases
* Experience with code reviews, refactoring, and production migrations


**Engagement:** 
 Part-time, project-based expert evaluation work
   

  

**Work Type:** 
 Remote
   

  

 Contributors will design and evaluate realistic software engineering tasks, including bug resolution, feature implementation, refactoring/migration, and test generation. Work includes both creating complex coding scenarios and reviewing peer submissions for quality and accuracy.
   

  

 This is a project-based consultant role. Consultants will be paid on a per-project basis; hourly rates are estimates based on anticipated completion time. Consultants control their own schedule, provide their own tools, and may simultaneously provide services to other vendors/employers (subject to those vendors’ allowances).
   

  

**Responsibilities:**
 Contributors will:
   

  

* Design and implement multi-file coding tasks across bug fixing, feature development, refactoring, and testing
* Write clear natural-language specifications and reference implementations

**Expected Outcomes:**
