title: Senior Vulnerability Researcher
description:
*Want to influence the bigger picture? At Saab, you can realize amazing innovations that keep people and societies safe. Together, we can truly make an impact – and your part matters.*


Your role


We are looking for Vulnerability Researchers to join us at Digital Defense at the Digital Battlespace Solutions Business Unit.


In this role, you will be performing vulnerability research, conducting advanced forensic investigations and developing your own tools for analysis and automation. You’ll identify, analyze, and understand security weaknesses in software and hardware. Report and demonstrate your findings with proof-of-concepts. During the task of mitigation, you’ll support as an advisor.


Your key responsibilities will be to:


* Vulnerability research: Perform research in order to find novel vulnerability flaws.
* Code reversal: Reverse engineer binaries and identify exploitable flaws.
* Develop and test access methods: Develop proof-of-concepts.
* Development of software tools: develop tools, automation and methods needed in the research.
* Reporting: Written and oral presentation of your research, inc. live demonstrations.


Your profile


You are highly motivated, analytical and technically skilled with deep expertise in code reversal, computer forensics and low-level programming.


Your skill set includes:

* Solid experience in analyzing code and binaries with tools such as IDA Pro, Ghidra, Binary Ninja, x64dbg or similar.
* Experience in analyzing technical vulnerabilities in software and hardware
* Strong knowledge of x86 and ARM architectures and operating systems (\*nix, windows, android).
* Good knowledge of low-level programming e.g. assembler and machine code.
* Ability to build and maintain tools for vulnerability research.
* Ability to communicate technical results clearly spoken and written English.


We value the following traits and experiences:

* As a researcher, you’re curious, clever and thinking outside the box.
* As a team member, you’re social, easygoing, helpful and like to share your knowledge.
* As a person, you’re mature, reliable and behaved.
* Experience in code reversal (reverse engineering) and binary patching.
* Experience in firmware analysis, UEFI/BIOS or embedded systems.
* Experience in kernel debugging.
* Experience in computer forensics.
* Understanding of malware behavior, obfuscation techniques and anti-analysis methods.
* Experience in exploit development, fuzzing, EDR/Antivirus bypassing or vulnerability analysis.
* Experience in red team operations and penetration testing.
* Knowledgeable in cyber threats.


This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.


What you will be a part of


Behind our innovations stand the people who make them possible. Brave pioneers and curious minds. Everyday heroes and inventive troubleshooters. Those who share deep knowledge and those who explore sky-high. And everyone in between.


Joining us means making an impact together, contributing in our own unique ways. From crafting complex code and building impressive defense and security solutions to simply sharing a coffee with a colleague, every action counts. We encourage you to take on challenges, to create smart inventions and grow in our friendly and tech-savvy workspace. We have a solid mission to keep people and society safe.


Saab is a leading defense and security company with an enduring mission, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 26,100 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.


Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defense capability of several nations. Read more about us here


Kindly observe that this is an ongoing recruitment process and that the position might be filled before the closing date of the advertisement.
company_description: Saab serves the global market with world-leading products, services and solutions from military defense to civil security.

title: Fullstack Engineer
description:
We put items to better use. Hundred thousand a week actually. Enhancing our technical product is therefore at the top of our agenda.
  
The scale of our operations creates complex, fascinating challenges. We thrive on engineering simple, elegant solutions to tackle them, and we are looking for someone whose craft can make a tangible impact on our rapidly growing customer base.
  
Sellpy’s vision is to empower everyone to live circularly. We believe that every item deserves a second life, and we see huge financial, social and environmental value in making sure this happens. We look for those who share this vision and have the grit to tackle the challenges of building a circular future.
  
Fullstack Engineer at Sellpy
  
Sellpy serves both buyers and sellers, and both sides matter equally. As part of Sellpy Tech, you'll help hundreds of thousands of customers embrace a smarter, more sustainable way to shop. This is work that actually moves the needle on something that matters.
  
We're looking for a Fullstack Engineer who cares about the customer experience and wants to push our technical product forward. This role is a great fit if you want to go deep on JavaScript, enjoy technical discussions, and thrive on real, varied challenges every day. Your ability to build features that integrate cleanly with our architecture and hold up in production is what makes the difference.
  
We code for a circular future — and we build it together. Debates are welcome, opinions are expected, and everyone has a real say in how we shape Sellpy's architecture.
  
In short, you will
  
Build user-centric, next-generation web applications
  
Work with JavaScript, React/React Native & Node across the stack
  
Take ownership of features from idea to release and beyond
  
Ensure scalability, maintainability and simplicity in everything you build
  
Continuously improve Sellpy’s products and architecture
  
  

Requirements
  
B.S. or M.S. in Computer Science (or related), or equivalent proven experience
  
3+ years of professional software engineering experience
  
Strong knowledge of JavaScript
  
Experience from a fast-paced product environment
  
Fluency in written and spoken English
  
  

We’d be impressed if you have
  
Experience with React, React Native, Node.js, Web Components, logging & monitoring
  
Worked in a scaling, product-focused startup
  
Hands-on experience with continuous integration & deployment
  
Knowledge of AWS and Git
  
  

You get to
  
Be part of a tech-minded company that's driving the change towards a sustainable lifestyle. In return, you will get direction, energy, and work on meaningful challenges daily.
  
Enjoy a vibrant office culture at Medborgarplatsen, balanced with hands-on experience at our production sites
  
Work with highly skilled and motivated colleagues
  
Gain new skills with unlimited access to a learning platform
  
Make use of prepaid vacation
  
Enjoy Sellpy credits and staff discounts at Sellpy and the H&M brands (Arket, COS, Other Stories, Weekday and more).
  
Save up with a monthly pension plan
  
Engagement in Sellpy’s social traditions with game nights, interactive planning days, guest speakers and other activities.
  
  

Location
  
On-site collaboration at our core, supported by a flexible hybrid setup on set days. This specific role is based at our Medborgarplatsen office.
  
Hybrid work
  
We value being together. After an on-site onboarding, we aim for a balanced office presence, with flexibility based on your team and tasks.
  
**Form of employment:** Full-time
  
**Start:** As agreed upon
  
Öppen för alla
  
Vi fokuserar på din kompetens, inte dina övriga förutsättningar. Vi är öppna för att anpassa rollen eller arbetsplatsen efter dina behov.

title: BIM Engineer
description:
At Endra, our vision is to produce software of exceptional quality. Attaining this standard requires both talent and ownership. We seek individuals who care deeply about the quality of the software we deliver, whether their focus is engineering, design, support, or sales.


Join our small and talented team as the technical link between our platform and our users. You’ll own features from concept to deployment, working directly with customers to shape the future of MEP design. We’re looking for engineers who love solving real-world problems, care about user experience, and are driven to see their code make an immediate impact.


You'll build the BIM integration layer that connects Endra's generative engine that engineers use every day, starting with Revit. You will define the architecture, set the standards, and directly shape how Endra's outputs land inside the real workflows of MEP firms worldwide. You will work shoulder-to-shoulder with our core engineering team, our product specialists, and the consultancies who will run our software on real projects.  

  

**What We're Looking For**

* 5+ years of professional software development with strong C#/.NET skills.
* Proven record of developing BIM tools, ideally revit addins.
* Experience integrating live BIM model data with external databases and APIs.
* Strong working knowledge of the Revit data model: families, parameters, worksharing, view templates, schedules, and documentation standards.
* Solid understanding of MEP workflows in at least one discipline (mechanical, electrical, plumbing, or fire protection).
* A systems-oriented, first-principles approach to software architecture in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.
* Ownership mindset. You don't just build what's asked, you take responsibility for the outcome.

**Nice to Haves**

* IFC and OpenBIM standards experience, or multi-platform BIM environments.
* Experience with Python and TypeScript.
* Experience in desktop development using WPF.
* Experience in Autodesk Platform Services.
* Computational geometry, graph algorithms, or generative design background.
* Exposure to automated testing and CI/CD for CAD/BIM tooling.
* Prior experience at a construction tech,AEC software company or engineering consultancy firm.
* Comfort working directly with technical customers (engineers and design firms).

**What You'll Do**

* Engineer Endra's Revit integration end-to-end, from add-in infrastructure to output fidelity.
* Translate Endra's generative outputs into clean, compliant, production-ready Revit models that meet firm-specific templates and jurisdictional requirements.
* Collaborate with our Core engineering team (C++23 and Python on GCP) on the boundary between the generative engine and the BIM layer.
* Define and uphold the quality bar for our BIM output across disciplines and project types.
* Work directly with customers during rollouts to understand workflow requirements and resolve integration issues.
* Build the foundations for Endra's BIM layer to scale across platforms, disciplines, and global markets.
* Help establish internal BIM standards, execution frameworks, and engineering best practices that the next BIM hires will inherit.

**Our Current Tech Stack**

* Core Logic (C++23): A high-performance, modern C++23 codebase developed from scratch. It underpins our system with geometric primitives, advanced optimization algorithms, and the core business logic essential for our MEP decision-making and validation frameworks.
* Backend Services (Python / GCP): A lightweight asynchronous Python API deployed on GCP. It manages database interactions (Postgres), generates required reports, and interfaces with our high-speed C++ Core library.
* Frontend Interface (Svelte / TypeScript / WebAssembly): A compact, high-performance CAD application delivered directly in the user's browser, built using Svelte, custom TypeScript, and WebAssembly components.
* BIM Layer (the one you'll own): Native Revit integration today, with IFC and multi-platform exports on the near-term roadmap.

**How we operate**

* Less is more: We simplify and ship smaller, fast.
* Build the right things: We listen carefully and build what users actually need.
* Velocity & Quality: We build things that last, but we don’t perfect them forever.
* Trust & Collaboration: Strong opinions, loosely held.

**What We Offer**

* Competitive salary and equity.
* Employee-friendly equity terms.
* Private health insurance.
* Regular team events and off-sites.
* Six weeks of paid vacation.
* An inspiring, ambitious, and global work environment.

**Ready to move the needle?**


Do you feel this is your opportunity to do your most meaningful work and reshape an entire industry? Let's talk!

title: HPC Field Support Engineer
description:
Bull is a story. One with a century of European innovation and a working environment where experts design powerful, sustainable, and sovereign digital solutions, enabling states and industries to retain full control over their data and their AI.  



Bull is also thousands of engineers, researchers and passionate tech people shaping the future of high‑performance computing, AI, and quantum technologies.  



Every day, our teams push the boundaries of what is technologically possible – from next‑generation HPC architectures to exascale supercomputers – supported by world‑class R&D, more than 1,600 patents, and unique end‑to‑end capabilities spanning hardware design, software engineering, data science and quantum research.  



We are a people‑centric, innovation‑driven company, where collaboration spans Europe, the Americas and India. We share a common vision of a responsible and sustainable innovation that delivers concrete impact for our customers.  



We are searching for an **HPC Field Support Engineer** to support a key customer on-site in **Linköping, Sweden**.  


**Role description:**

In this role, you will work directly from the customer’s data centre, supporting the installation, maintenance, and day-to-day operation of advanced HPC/AI systems. You will act as the primary interface between the customer and backline support teams, ensuring smooth operations and efficient issue resolution.

You will gain exposure to the full lifecycle of HPC/AI environments, including hardware, system software, and infrastructure support, while developing hands-on technical expertise.

This is a great opportunity for candidates early in their careers to gain hands-on experience in hardware support, Linux systems, and HPC environments, while working directly with a key customer in a production setting.  



**Responsibilities:**
* Provide on-site technical support for HPC/AI systems in a customer data centre
* Perform hardware maintenance and parts replacement (servers, components, etc.)
* Deliver Level 1 system software support, primarily in Linux environments
* Troubleshoot hardware, OS, and basic network-related issues
* Act as the interface between the customer and Bull’s support teams
* Support system installations, upgrades, and lifecycle activities
* Participate periodically in HPC/AI deployments across Europe
* Ensure high system availability and contribute to continuous improvement of support processes

  



**Skills & Competencies:**
* Hands-on experience with computer hardware (build, repair, maintenance)
* Good working knowledge of Linux systems
* Basic shell scripting skills
* Understanding of networking fundamentals and network security
* Strong technical interest in computing infrastructure and engineering
* Strong communication skills, able to explain technical topics clearly
* Confident working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
* Good interpersonal skills and ability to build trust with customers
* Problem-solving mindset with curiosity and willingness to learn
* Well-organised with strong attention to detail

  



**Why apply?**

This is a great opportunity to develop your **hardware and system software engineering skills** while gaining hands-on experience working closely with customer technical teams to maintain high-end HPC/AI systems.

As part of a comprehensive technical training program, you will receive practical training in **system hardware maintenance, HPC cluster management software, and high-performance storage**, helping you build a strong foundation in high-performance computing environments.  



**What We Offer:**
* Hands-on experience with cutting-edge HPC and AI systems
* Structured technical training (hardware, cluster management, high-performance storage)
* Exposure to the full HPC/AI lifecycle
* A dynamic and international working environment
* Opportunities for professional growth and long-term career development
* A collaborative and supportive team culture
* Access to a strong technical network to support your development in HPC engineering
  



**Join us!**  



Here, your ideas, your curiosity and your technical excellence directly shape the next era of advanced computing - unlocking enterprise value, accelerating scientific progress and driving positive impact for society.
company_description: Across the world, Atos enables its customers and employees, and members of societies at large to live, work and develop sustainably, in a safe and secure information space.

title: Hardware Engineer
description:
**Do you want to work hands-on with FPGA and ASIC development while designing processor and peripheral IP cores used in space and other safety-critical systems? At Frontgrade Gaisler, you will work close to the hardware, contributing to advanced system-on-chip solutions used in demanding environments for years to come.**


We are looking for a Hardware Engineer with a strong interest in RTL development to join our experienced team in central Gothenburg.

**What you will do**


As a Hardware Engineer, you will participate in the complete development process of system-on-chip designs based on our IP cores, from architectural design, development and verification, to implementation and prototype validation.

**Your responsibilities will include:**

* Development of RTL models for IP cores and system-on-chip designs using VHDL
* Verification of designs and IP cores through simulation using VHDL and/or SystemVerilog
* FPGA prototyping and hardware validation of designs
* Implementation in target technologies, including both ASIC and FPGA
* Maintenance and development of our IP core portfolio and supporting infrastructure
* Validation, testing and measurements on completed devices
* Creation of clear and comprehensive technical documentation
* Providing technical support to customers and the sales organization

**Who we believe you are**


You enjoy working close to the architecture and care about clean, robust, and reusable design. You like diving deep into technical details and understanding how your work fits into larger systems.

**We believe you have**:

* A M.Sc. or Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field
* At least 3 years of experience in RTL design and verification
* Strong skills in VHDL (or a similar HDL)
* The ability to write basic embedded software in C
* Experience using scripting languages such as TCL or Python

**It is a strong advantage if you have experience with:**

* System-on-chip design centered around on-chip buses such as AMBA AHB or AXI
* Computer architecture, microprocessor design, RISC-V, SPARC
* Hands-on lab work with hardware equipment

**Why join Frontgrade Gaisler?**

* Work with advanced hardware solutions used in real space missions
* Long-term, technically deep projects with high quality requirements
* Hybrid work model, up to two days per week remote
* Private health care insurance and regular health check-ups
* Annual wellness allowance of 5000 SEK
* Central office location

**About us**


We are a world leader in embedded computer systems for harsh environments, with footprints in several parts of the solar system. We make a real difference when providing cutting edge products to support digital hardware design for mission critical solutions for space. Our customer base is worldwide in an exciting and growing market. When other companies say they "Think global, and Act local", we work on a much larger scale. What we develop is to be used in space and benefit humanity on Earth, thus we "Act local, but Think on a solar system scale”

title: Embedded QA Engineer
description:
**About Justera Group**  

Justera Group AB is a leading Swedish IT consulting firm with great experience specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. Led by dynamic female executives, Justera Group focuses on helping businesses harness the power of digital technologies to continuously evolve in today’s rapidly changing economy. The company has deep expertise in IT consultancy, software development, and recruitment services, providing clients with purpose-driven, well-trained consultants who consistently deliver efficient and effective results. Justera Group manages the entire talent lifecycle, from hiring to ongoing management, ensuring the right fit for each project on time and within budget. With their strong commitment to quality, collaboration, and client success, Justera Group is a trusted partner for technology innovation across Sweden.

 **About the Role:**  

Justera is currently recruiting an **Embedded QA Engineer (Mid-Level)** on behalf of one of our key clients in the Gaming industry.


Our client is seeking a collaborative, quality-driven QA professional to join their cross-functional development team in Stockholm. In this role, you won't just be finding bugs at the end of the line—you will be "shifting quality left." You will act as the core quality gatekeeper, working side-by-side with developers, artists, and producers to champion a quality-first mindset, assess risks early in the design phase, and ensure flawless feature releases on mobile platforms.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* **Drive Quality Culture:** Promote and adapt QA methodologies within your team, encouraging shared quality ownership and challenging the status quo where needed.
* **Risk & Compliance Management:** Identify and flag risks, blockers, dependencies, and legal/platform compliance issues early in the feature and content development process.
* **Hands-on Testing:** Perform manual, exploratory, and technical testing across multiple mobile platforms, seamlessly integrating into the team’s GitHub PR flow.
* **Test Documentation:** Create, maintain, and update test cases, test plans, and test scripts.
* **Bug Lifecycle Ownership:** Oversee the end-to-end lifecycle of game bugs using Jira, and coordinate both internal and external test sessions.
* **Cross-Craft Collaboration:** Act as the strategic QA point of contact, aligning with Team Producers, Project QA Leads, developers, and artists to guide data-driven decision-making.
* **Live Ops Support:** Partner with stakeholders on game releases, post-release monitoring, and community issue triaging alongside customer service.

**Mandatory Requirements:**

* **Games Industry Experience:** 1+ years of professional QA experience specifically within gaming, possessing a solid grasp of game development cycles and release flows.
* **Technical & Engine Skills:** Practical, hands-on experience working inside the **Unity Editor** and familiarity with developer consoles.
* **Mobile Platform Knowledge:** Solid understanding of iOS and Android hardware, operating systems, and platform-specific behaviors.
* **Tools Proficiency:** Proven experience utilizing **Jira** for bug tracking and **GitHub** for PR deployment flows.
* **Communication:** Full professional fluency in **English** with excellent interpersonal skills to communicate effectively across different crafts (engineers, artists, product owners).
* **Mindset:** An exceptional eye for detail and a proactive approach to risk assessment.

**Nice to Have:**

* Basic programming or coding knowledge, with an interest in learning/supporting test automation requirements and writing effective test scripts.
* A basic understanding of client-server relationships.
* A deep, genuine passion for technology and video games.

**Logistics:**

* **Start Date:** ASAP
* **Duration:** 4–6 months (with a strong possibility of extension to 6+ months)
* **Location:** Stockholm
* **Setup:** **Hybrid (requiring on-site attendance 3 days per week)**

**What We Offer:**

* A dynamic and innovative workplace driving advancements in cutting-edge robotic technologies
* The opportunity to collaborate with talented cross-functional teams on meaningful and impactful projects
* Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package
* A supportive culture that fosters continuous learning, growth, and professional development
* Flexible hybrid working model for an optimal work-life balance
* 25 days of annual leave to recharge and relax
* An annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK to support your health and wellbeing

title: Test Automation Engineer - Climate & Tunnel Systems
description:
**Let's introduce ourselves**
-----------------------------



Do you want to work in some of the world’s most advanced test environments and contribute to the development of next‑generation vehicles?

  



We are now looking for a Test Automation Engineer for our high‑tech wind tunnel systems. Here, you will combine mechatronics, embedded systems, test automation, and hands‑on laboratory work in an environment where innovation and technical development are at the core.

  




We work in an advanced and dynamic technical environment where mechatronic systems, embedded controls and sophisticated hardware come together to create highly realistic testing conditions. Our tunnel testing operations support a wide range of development activities and play a crucial role in ensuring reliable and accurate results.

  




The organization is built around close collaboration, continuous improvement and a strong commitment to highquality testing that enables futureproof propulsion solutions.

  



Today we are 20 engineers and technicians with various backgrounds ensuring 16/5 operation of our wind and climatic test cells.

**What you'll do**
------------------



In this position, you will work in advanced laboratory environments focusing on vehicles, climate and wind tunnel systems.

  



You will be part of a team ensuring that testing is performed with correct methodology, accurate data, and the highest quality.

  



Your responsibilities include:


* Planning and driving development activities within the TAS (Test Automation System) domain
* Developing, implementing, and maintaining features and methods in test cell and tunnel systems
* Configuring software in test cell control systems and performing troubleshooting on complex HW/SW systems
* Working with data handling, data processing, and pipelines supporting the test operations
* Assessing the plausibility of test results using measurement uncertainty and analytical methods
* Contributing to future technical decisions and strategies for automated test systems
* Building networks within R&D and the laboratory organization
**What you'll bring**
---------------------



You are analytical, solution‑oriented, and self‑driven, with a natural desire to understand and improve complex systems. You enjoy hands‑on work in laboratory environments, take responsibility, and collaborate well with others.



We believe you have:


* M.Sc. or B.Sc. in Automation, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechatronics, or equivalent
* Experience in HW verification, test method development, or system testing
* Programming experience in embedded systems, C, Python, and familiarity with multiple development environments
* Knowledge of Communication protocol like UDP, TCP, CAN and network design as well as measurement devices
* Experience with test automation systems (meritorious)
* Ability to quickly learn new tools, languages, and technical concepts
* Strong communication skills in English and Swedish

  



Personal characteristics:


* Comfortable making decisions and taking initiative
* Structured and improvement‑oriented
* Curious, technically driven, and eager to learn
* Cooperative and engaged in the team’s success

  



OTHER INFORMATION:


* A valid driver’s license (B) is required
* Shift work (day or day/evening) may occur

  



APPLICATION:



Don’t wait — selection is ongoing!



If you have questions about the position, please contact the hiring manager.

**Volvo Cars. For Life.**  

For nearly a century, Volvo Cars has empowered people to move freely in a personal, sustainable and safe way. Today, we are driving bold advancements in electrification, sustainability and automotive safety. To realise our ambitious vision, we are seeking innovative minds who are ready to tackle the challenges of tomorrow – today.  

  

In our company, we believe extraordinary things are achieved by ordinary people with the drive to make a difference.  

  

**Ready to take the next step?**  

Submit your CV in English and tell us why you’re the ideal candidate for a new role at Volvo Cars. Applications must be received no later than June 4th, 2026 . We are prioritising direct applications to ensure a fair and efficient application process.  

  

For questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Recruiter Mark H Chan at MARK.CHAN@VOLVOCARS.COM. For specific questions about the position, please reach out to Hiring Manager Ann Westerberg at ANN.WESTERBERG@VOLVOCARS.COM.  

  

As part of the recruitment process, the final candidates might undergo a background check.  

  

**We welcome your application!**

title: Principal Software Engineer
description:
**DESCRIPTION** 




  





 Sinch Telco is a standalone business within Sinch. Our products propel communications services for mobile operators and enterprises around the globe. We are looking for a Principal Software Engineer with focus on AI-driven Development, to accelerate adoption of AI-powered development practices across Sinch Telco.
 



  





 In this role, you'll be a hands-on practitioner, leveraging AI agents to design, build and deliver software while shaping how AI is applied in real engineering environments. Together we will move toward a paradigm where software developers manage teams of specialized virtual AI agents for requirements analysis, implementation, testing, documentation, and deployment. We intend to unlock new capabilities that enable teams to build and ship faster with higher quality, security, and maintainability.
 



  





 You see the future of AI-driven development clearly but understand that progress requires practical steps and patience. You demonstrate value through hands-on work and tangible results. You collaborate well with experienced colleagues as a peer and respect existing domain knowledge while contributing new perspectives and influence through quality of work. You are self-directed, proactive, and comfortable operating with ambiguity. The AI tooling landscape changes rapidly and you are energized by this pace.
 



  





 As our Principal Software Engineer, you will:
 


* Act as a highly productive, hands-on software engineer using AI agent orchestration to deliver features and products across multiple areas
* Define, evolve, and scale best practices, workflows, and tooling for AI-assisted development across coding, testing, documentation, and deployment.
* Evaluate, select, and configure emerging AI development tools and agent frameworks.
* Create reusable agent configurations, custom instructions, and workflows tailored to the organization's codebases.
* Partner with engineers, architects, and product managers to define clear requirements and product direction using a product management-informed perspective.



  





**REQUIREMENTS** 



* 8+ years of software engineering experience with proven ability to design, evaluate, and evolve software architectures.
* Solid understanding of distributed systems, APIs, and system integration. Experience with telecom systems, BSS/OSS, messaging infrastructure, or CPaaS is a plus.
* Hands-on experience applying AI-assisted development tools such as Copilot, Cursor, Claude, in real-world software delivery, not just experimentation.
* Experience working with AI-driven and agent-based development workflows, including autonomous coding agents and multistep task orchestration.
* Ability to define clear, structured, and decomposable requirements for both engineers and AI agents, with strong product awareness and understanding of the "why" behind solutions.
* Practical experience with Model Context Protocol, tool-use patterns, or equivalent integration standards for reliable multi-agent/tool orchestration. Experience with multi-agent frameworks such as CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph is a plus.
* Strong communication skills, with the ability to articulate technical concepts, document approaches, and collaborate effectively across teams.



  





 Our corporate language is English, please submit your application in English.
 



 We are not supporting relocation at this time. Applicants must either hold a Swedish or EU/EEA citizenship or have a valid Swedish work permit.
 



  





**OUR HIRING PROCESS** 




  





 We are committed to ensuring a recruitment process that is fair, objective, consistent, and inclusive.
 



  





 We believe in a two-way process and encourage you to ask questions throughout the journey. If this role isn't what you're looking for, please explore the other opportunities listed on our career page: https://www.sinch.com/careers/. No matter who you are, we hope you find an exciting path forward - hopefully with us!

title: Controls Software Design Engineer
